title: RFE: Squash images down to a single UID/GID when pulling
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

In environments where user namespaces are not possible (#3478), the `--uidmap` flag is useful for running a container with a single UID/GID as a workaround for these scenarios (more context in #3561).

Currently, `podman pull` fails if there are not enough UIDs/GIDs allocated on the system:

```sh
$ podman --log-level=debug pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest
[…]
DEBU[0005] Start untar layer                            
ERRO[0006] Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 0:35 for /usr/libexec/utempter): lchown /usr/libexec/utempter: invalid argument 
ERRO[0006] Error pulling image ref //registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:fdc986defe1731a1baf2f3720228be2992307c5e1eb817c3b146daafed7cea40": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 0:35 for /usr/libexec/utempter): lchown /usr/libexec/utempter: invalid argument 
Failed
ERRO[0006] error pulling image "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest": unable to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest: unable to pull image: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:fdc986defe1731a1baf2f3720228be2992307c5e1eb817c3b146daafed7cea40": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 0:35 for /usr/libexec/utempter): lchown /usr/libexec/utempter: invalid argument
```

This is a chicken-and-egg scenario. I cannot pull containers to the host even if I will use `--uidmap 0:0:1` when running the container.

@mheon mentioned adding support for Podman to tell containers/storage to to squash an image down to a single UID/GID if they are not allocated on the system. This would enable us to pull any image, vs. building custom images with a manual `chown` step added in the build instructions.

For what it is worth, I did bind-mount a NFS path in as a volume and read/wrote to it in the container with the `--uidmap` flag in Podman v1.4.5-dev, so this use case is already possible in `podman run`. I have not tested with GPFS yet though because of this issue.

Any thoughts?

comments.body: @giuseppe @nalind @rhatdan PTAL
it is being addressed here: https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/387
@jwflory could you build a podman using that version of containers/storage and see if it solves your problem?

@giuseppe I built Podman with @rhatdan's patch but from what I could tell, the feature needs to be enabled. There was no change when running the same command in the original comment. Is there a step I should follow to use the overlay flag?
Can you please include the output you get?
any chance to get the PR merged? Having currently lot's of problems due to the image squashing issue

> any chance to get the PR merged? Having currently lot's of problems due to the image squashing issue

@hypery2k we are still testing it, and it will hopefully merged soon.  Even with that in place, it is still suggested to use multiple IDs inside of a user namespace, as it doesn't require to use a modified image.
totally with you, but I didn't get it working on OpenShift 3.11

@giuseppe @rhatdan This is what I currently get with the c/storage patch. The Podman package itself I am using includes up to commit ade0d87:

`podman --log-level=debug --storage-opt overlay.ignore_chown_errors=true pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest`

seeing the same error

It is working for me with this command

```
$ /tmp/podman --storage-opt overlay.ignore_chown_errors=true --storage-opt overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs  pull fedora
Trying to pull docker.io/library/fedora...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 07b72cd62760 skipped: already exists
Copying config 2b74bf3d24 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
2b74bf3d2430a5a44ffa180012989017eccd74c1cff3e3c000d7377e3670e354

$ /tmp/podman --storage-opt overlay.ignore_chown_errors=true --storage-opt overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs  run -ti fedora cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0       3269          1
```
[storage.patch.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/3421956/storage.patch.txt)
I noticed you are using `fuse-overlayfs`. We are using the `vfs` storage driver in our environment since our kernel doesn't support `fuse-overlayfs`. We are currently on RHEL 7.5.
@rhatdan Can you upload the podman binary on google drive or dropbox? Want to give a try and having issue with building the PR

@jwflory we would need to have a different patch to use this on VFS.  I will look into that.
@hypery2k Which OS?
centos/rhel would be great
Struggling with the VFS patch right now...

> > any chance to get the PR merged? Having currently lot's of problems due to the image squashing issue
> 
> @hypery2k we are still testing it, and it will hopefully merged soon. Even with that in place, it is still suggested to use multiple IDs inside of a user namespace, as it doesn't require to use a modified image.

@giuseppe Could you help me here how to do this on OpenShift 3.11?  Is there a mailing list or chat to discuss this further?
so what is the solution on rhel/centos-7.7 ?
Probably nothing until podman 1.6 arrives.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@rhatdan, this is working now, is it?
Should arrive in RHEL/Centos 7.8  with podman 1.6.*
